slides.title: 01 // COLLECTIVE MEMORY
slides.body:
Memory is not a static archive, but an unstable process of encoding, retrieval, and decay; it shifts, collapses, and rewrites itself constantly. In the last decade or so, 'artificial intelligence' has been exposed as a reflection of how memory operates, and within this operation are its inherent biases, fractures, and capacity for curiosity and invention.

Architecture contains memory. Spaces contain memories, old walls are torn down, types of architectural witness marks are left, with tooling marks and bits of material leftovers. Many of these are hidden below the surface, covered by fresh gypsum and spackle, left to only be discovered by the next entity that tears down these walls.

These collisions of old and new produce moments of dissonance, where architectural time collapses into a single frame.

slides.title: 02 // BOTTEGA LOUIE: SYNTHESIS
slides.body:
Bottega Louie's Downtown LA location is an architectural spectacle, designed to immerse and transport. Its soaring white walls and high ceilings cultivate an "old world gorgeous" luxury, amplified by the cacophony of voices that echo through the vast, open space, creating an energetic "Italian glow." The expansive bakery display, a "world class" introduction of artful macarons and pastries, serves as a visual and aromatic antechamber, instantly signifying an escape into a "bougie" culinary theater.

This grand setting actively constructs memory, becoming a backdrop for cherished family traditions, romantic celebrations, and impressive gatherings with out-of-town guests. Visitors recall not just the food, but the unforgettable "experience" – from the luxury of the bathrooms to the transformation felt upon entry. Whether a spontaneous brunch or a milestone birthday, Bottega Louie consistently delivers an atmosphere so potent and transporting, it imprints itself deeply into personal history, inviting return visits across years.

slides.title: 03 // O.T. JOHNSON: SYNTHESIS
slides.body:
The O. T. Johnson Building stands as a Romanesque anchor in the pulsating Broadway Theater District of Downtown Los Angeles, its very presence an atmospheric echo of a century of urban drama. Its initial design speaks of a bygone era, yet its material form registers the continuous layering of time, adaptation, and human activity, manifesting a profound sense of temporal depth.

This structure is a tangible vessel for collective memory, a palimpsest where each historical modification, from original construction to modern alterations, etches new narratives onto its architectural skin. It embodies an evolving civic identity, deeply interwoven with the vibrant, ever-changing pulse of one of Los Angeles' most significant urban cores.
